steps.title: Set the horizontal value to zero
steps.content:
The $y$-intercept is where the graph crosses the $y$-axis. At that point, the horizontal coordinate is $0$, so substitute $x=0$ into the equation.
$$y=\frac{45}{0-5}$$

steps.title: Evaluate the coordinate
steps.content:
Simplify the fraction to find the vertical coordinate. The intercept point uses $x=0$ together with this value of $y$.
$$y=\frac{45}{-5}=-9$$
The $y$-intercept is $(0,-9)$. The answer is A.
